metadata.summary: Spatial computing platforms, terminology, and market context. AR/VR/MR distinctions, platform landscape (Vision Pro, Quest, Android XR, HoloLens, Magic Leap), and per-platform design implications.

content.title: Spatial Computing Overview
content.summary: Reference overview of spatial computing platforms, terminology, and market context for designers entering AR/VR/MR work. Covers the distinction between AR, VR, and MR; the current platform landscape (Apple Vision Pro, Meta Quest, Android XR, HoloLens, Magic Leap); and the design implications of 3D-space interfaces versus rectangular screens.

why.p: Designers coming from 2D backgrounds often conflate AR, VR, and MR, leading to wrong platform assumptions, incorrect interaction model selection, and deliverables that do not match the target device's capabilities. A shared vocabulary and platform map prevents misaligned design decisions early in a project.

list.item: Onboarding to a spatial computing project with no prior XR design experience
Choosing which XR platform to target for a given use case
Writing briefs or technical specs that need precise AR/VR/MR terminology
Evaluating market context and platform maturity before committing to a platform
Deep-dive on specific interaction patterns — use the spatial-interaction-patterns methodology instead
Tooling decisions — use the spatial-design-tools methodology instead
Enterprise XR requirements — use the enterprise-xr-applications methodology instead

rule.statement: Use precise XR terminology in all design documentation: AR (digital overlay on real world, passthrough camera or optical), VR (fully immersive synthetic environment), MR (real and digital objects interact in real time), XR (umbrella term for all three).
rule.rationale: Conflating AR and VR leads to wrong platform selection, incorrect interaction model assumptions, and deliverables that cannot be implemented on the target device.

list.item: Apple Vision Pro: spatial computing (MR), premium UX, enterprise-ready, visionOS, primary input via eye+hand+voice, no controllers
Meta Quest 3/Pro: VR and color passthrough MR, largest consumer install base, controller and hand tracking, Android XR SDK
Android XR: open platform for Android ecosystem devices, largest potential scale, early maturity in 2026
Microsoft HoloLens 2: AR headset, enterprise and industrial focus, spatial mapping, gaze+hand tracking, Windows Mixed Reality
Magic Leap 2: AR for industrial applications, high brightness for factory floors, enterprise-only
AR/VR headset market: 87% projected growth in 2026
40% of Global 2000 manufacturers have deployed digital twin initiatives
Enterprise is the primary revenue driver; consumer adoption lags but accelerating with Quest and Android XR

rule.statement: For Apple Vision Pro: design eye-gaze + pinch as the primary interaction; no physical controller grip states; use visionOS window and volume primitives; assume high rendering quality and precision eye tracking.
rule.rationale: visionOS was designed around eye+hand input from the ground up; controller-centric patterns from other platforms do not translate and will feel unnatural.

rule.statement: For Meta Quest: design for both hand tracking and controller input as parallel paths; assume color passthrough for MR use cases; test comfort for the larger consumer user base including non-technical users.
rule.rationale: Quest's broad consumer install base includes users unfamiliar with 3D interfaces; simpler interaction models and robust onboarding are required.

rule.statement: For HoloLens and Magic Leap: design for factory and field use — glove compatibility, outdoor brightness, short interaction sequences between physical work tasks, voice as primary input when hands are occupied.
rule.rationale: Industrial XR users are performing physical tasks concurrently; interaction must be minimal and glove-compatible; content must be visible in high-ambient-light environments.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Use this overview to seed a scoping conversation, then route to specific methodologies. Agents help with: platform-comparison matrix, market-context-2026 fact-check, AR-vs-VR-vs-MR decision tree from a use case, deck/document drafts. Agents do NOT replace: hands-on device evaluation, user interviews, ergonomic testing.

list.item: `faion-sdd-executor-agent` — translate scoping decisions into SDD spec/design tasks per platform target.
Platform-decision subagent — given use case + constraints (mobile/seated/social/industrial), recommend AR / VR / MR + specific device.
Market-fact subagent — when README cites figures, verify against current sources and replace stale data; flag when sources older than 6 months.

section.name: prompt-patterns
section.code: Given this use case: "{{description}}", recommend AR vs VR vs MR plus a device shortlist (Vision Pro / Quest 3 / Quest 3S / HoloLens / Magic Leap 2 / Android XR / WebXR / Pico). For each, give one-sentence rationale and one constraint that could disqualify. End with a single primary recommendation and a fallback.

Build a 2026 spatial-computing landscape brief (≤500 words): - AR / VR / MR definitions with examples. - Top 5 platforms with strengths and one weakness each. - Two trends to watch (e.g., smart glasses, generative content). - Cite at least three sources from the last 6 months.

list.item: Overview-only: ≈40 lines, no implementation detail.
Market figures (87% growth) age fast; verify before quoting.
Platform list is incomplete — Pico, Lynx, Varjo (industrial), Bigscreen Beyond, Samsung's headset all relevant in 2026.
Doesn't cover WebXR, smart glasses (Ray-Ban Meta, Halliday, Brilliant Labs Frame), or mobile AR.
"Spatial computing" is contested terminology — Apple's framing vs. Meta's "MR" vs. Microsoft's "MR" differ.
Unity XR Interaction Toolkit — Cross-platform XR. Docs: https://docs.unity3d.com/Packages/com.unity.xr.interaction.toolkit
Unreal OpenXR — Cross-platform. Docs: https://docs.unrealengine.com/5.0/en-US/openxr-in-unreal-engine/
MRTK3 — Microsoft mixed-reality toolkit. Repo: https://github.com/MixedRealityToolkit/MixedRealityToolkit-Unity
visionOS SDK / RealityKit / Reality Composer Pro — Vision Pro native. Docs: https://developer.apple.com/visionos/
Meta XR SDK + Presence Platform — Quest features. Docs: https://developer.oculus.com
ARKit / RealityKit (iOS) — Mobile AR. Docs: https://developer.apple.com/augmented-reality/
ARCore — Android AR. Docs: https://developers.google.com/ar
WebXR — Browser XR. Spec: https://immersive-web.github.io/webxr/
Babylon.js / Three.js + WebXR — OSS web XR. Sites: https://www.babylonjs.com / https://threejs.org
Apple App Store / Reality Composer — Vision Pro distribution (manual).
Meta Quest Store / App Lab / Quest for Business — Consumer + enterprise channels (manual + API).
Microsoft Store on HoloLens / Intune — Enterprise distribution (API).
Niantic Lightship — Real-world AR (geo, semantic). Website: https://lightship.dev
8thWall (Niantic) — Web AR, no-app-required. SaaS with SDK.
Snap Lens Studio + Spectacles — AR glasses (Spectacles 5). Platform with SDK.
Sketchfab / Polycam / Luma AI — 3D asset capture and library. SaaS with API.
NVIDIA Omniverse / USD — Industrial 3D collaboration. SaaS with API.
Stale facts: market growth rates, install bases, and platform roadmaps change quarterly. Agents quoting README numbers without verification will mislead stakeholders.
LLM tends to over-recommend Vision Pro for premium use cases; balance with cost and install-base realities (Vision Pro install base remains small in 2026).
Confusion between "MR" terms — Apple "passthrough", Meta "MR", Microsoft "MR" are not equivalent. Agent must specify which.
Agents may suggest WebXR as a universal answer; acknowledge platform-specific limits (no Vision Pro WebXR full feature parity, no advanced hand tracking on iOS Safari).
Don't let agent design specific interactions from this overview alone — it lacks the depth.
Agents may forget about smart glasses, treating "spatial" = headsets only; Ray-Ban Meta and similar need consideration in 2026.
Treat as a router: when an agent reaches a decision point, dispatch to the deeper methodology rather than answering inline.
Agents may underweight ergonomics and battery — flag both as scoping checks.
Start every spatial project with this overview, then immediately go deeper — do not stay at this level for design decisions.
Pick OpenXR-aligned tooling for forward portability across vendors.
Validate against actual hardware early — emulators lie about ergonomics, FOV, and tracking quality.
Match interaction model to platform strengths: hand-eye on Vision Pro, controllers + hands on Quest, hands + voice on HoloLens.
Cover accessibility (`spatial-accessibility`) from the start — retrofitting is expensive in XR.
Plan content distribution (App Store, Quest Store, Intune) and revenue/licensing model in scoping, not post-build.
Beware "spatial computing" hype-claims; ground decisions in measurable user value.
